docs.title: Welcome
docs.text:
Welcome to the documentation.

Use the navigation on the left to explore guides.

docs.title: ECML - Enforcer Custom Mission Loader
docs.text: A simple mod for Enforcer: Police Crime Action bringing capability for creating custom missions.

docs.title: Creating a new mission:
docs.text:
1. Inside the Mods folder, go into the Missions folder. 2. Create a new mission file, name it in any way you want and end it with ".xml".

Mission naming syntax

Our naming syntax was CM_ for custom missions and GM_ for game missions but you can ignore it.

3. Open the file in your text editor of choice and paste the following template: CM_MissionTemplate.xml

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<Mission>
    <MissionID></MissionID>
    <Scene></Scene>
    <RadioMessage></RadioMessage>
    <MissionID2></MissionID2>
    <MissionMarked></MissionMarked>
    <FalseAlarm></FalseAlarm>
    <HasVictim></HasVictim>
    <VictimPanicking></VictimPanicking>
    <VictimState></VictimState>
    <HowInjured></HowInjured>
    <DrunkVictim></DrunkVictim>
    <ShowVictimOnMap></ShowVictimOnMap>
    <HasCuriousNPC></HasCuriousNPC>
    <SuspectDescription></SuspectDescription>
    <NoDetails></NoDetails>
    <HasCriminal></HasCriminal>
    <ChanceToCooperate></ChanceToCooperate>
    <CriminalPanicking></CriminalPanicking>
    <CriminalState></CriminalState>
    <HowInjured2></HowInjured2>
    <DrunkCriminal></DrunkCriminal>
    <HasStolenGoods></HasStolenGoods>
    <HasMeleeWeapon></HasMeleeWeapon>
    <HoldsMeleeWeapon></HoldsMeleeWeapon>
    <HasGun></HasGun>
    <HoldsGun></HoldsGun>
    <WontMove></WontMove>
    <HasDrugs></HasDrugs>
    <IsDrugDealer></IsDrugDealer>
    <IsWanted></IsWanted>
</Mission>
4. Fill in the gaps in the xml file based on those tips:

MissionIDSceneRadioMessageMissionID2MissionMarkedFalseAlarmHasVictimVictimPanickingVictimStateHowInjuredDrunkVictimShowVictimOnMapHasCuriousNPCSuspectDescriptionNoDetailsHasCriminalChanceToCooperateCriminalPanickingCriminalStateHowInjured2

ID (from the list below) that dictates what kind of crime has been or is beeing commited and at what kind of place (in the game the places are specified by the crime).

Mission IDs

0 - none 1 - directDrunk 2 - directThief 3 - directVandalism 4 - directAssault 5 - directInjuredPerson 6 - directAccessory 7 - directBurglary 8 - directRobbery 9 - directDrugPossession 10 - directDrugTrafficking 11 - directKidnapping 12 - directMurder 13 - directDrugManufactoring 14 - directShoplifting 15 - directAccident

The scene in which the crime is able to take place (leave empty if it can happen in every scene).

Scenes

MapCenter MapSouth MapNorth MapNorthWest MapWest MapEast MapNorthEast

The radio message that plays when the mission starts (ID from the list below).

Mission IDs

0 - none 1 - directDrunk 2 - directThief 3 - directVandalism 4 - directAssault 5 - directInjuredPerson 6 - directAccessory 7 - directBurglary 8 - directRobbery 9 - directDrugPossession 10 - directDrugTrafficking 11 - directKidnapping 12 - directMurder 13 - directDrugManufactoring 14 - directShoplifting 15 - directAccident

ID (from the list below) that dictates what kind of criminal appears at the scene of crime.

Mission IDs

0 - none 1 - directDrunk 2 - directThief 3 - directVandalism 4 - directAssault 5 - directInjuredPerson 6 - directAccessory 7 - directBurglary 8 - directRobbery 9 - directDrugPossession 10 - directDrugTrafficking 11 - directKidnapping 12 - directMurder 13 - directDrugManufactoring 14 - directShoplifting 15 - directAccident

true or false (should the mission be marked on the map and in the world).

true or false (is mission a false alarm, false alarm missions make you just check out the place).

true or false (self explanatory).

true or false (panicking victims run away on sight).

The state of the victim when seen (pick on from the list below).

NPC States

0 - none 1 - _Criminal_SetOnTheLoose(); 2 - _Criminal_SetRunAwayFromVictim(); 3 - _Criminal_StartRobbingVictim(); 4 - _SetDEAD(); 5 - _SetINCAPACITATED(); 6 - _SetINJURED(-1f); 7 - _Victim_SetWithFear(); 8 - _Victim_StartToBeenRobbed(); 9 - _Victim_StartToKeepStanding();

If the state is injured, how injured (how much HP does the Victim have).

true or false (self explanatory).

true or false (should the victim appear on the minimap).

true or false (curious NPCs have info about the crime - SuspectDescription).

Self explanatory.

true or false (dictates whether the suspect has a description).

true or false (self explanatory).

0 - 100 the chance (percentage) for the criminal to cooperate with police.

true or false (panicking criminals run away on sight).

The state of the criminal when seen (pick on from the list below).

NPC States

0 - none 1 - _Criminal_SetOnTheLoose(); 2 - _Criminal_SetRunAwayFromVictim(); 3 - _Criminal_StartRobbingVictim(); 4 - _SetDEAD(); 5 - _SetINCAPACITATED(); 6 - _SetINJURED(-1f); 7 - _Victim_SetWithFear(); 8 - _Victim_StartToBeenRobbed(); 9 - _Victim_StartToKeepStanding();

If the state is injured, how injured (how much HP does the Criminal have).

From DrunkCriminal to IsWanted

All of these are self explanatory true or false.

5. Save the file if it looks like the example on the following page and have fun playing the game with your custom mission loaded!

docs.title: Follow these steps:
docs.text: 1. Get MelonLoader v0.5.7 (x86). 2. After unzipping the downloaded file directly into the game's folder, launch the game. 3. Now close the game and download the mod. 4. Unzip the mod's contents into the Mods folder inside the game's folder, if the Mods folder isn't there, create it. 5. You did it! Run the game and have fun!

docs.title: Example Mission
docs.text:
This mission file comes with the mod!

CM_ExampleCustomMission.xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<Mission>
    <MissionID>12</MissionID>
    <Scene></Scene>
    <RadioMessage>12</RadioMessage>
    <MissionID2>1</MissionID2>
    <MissionMarked>true</MissionMarked>
    <FalseAlarm>false</FalseAlarm>
    <HasVictim>true</HasVictim>
    <VictimPanicking>false</VictimPanicking>
    <VictimState>4</VictimState>
    <HowInjured></HowInjured>
    <DrunkVictim>false</DrunkVictim>
    <ShowVictimOnMap>false</ShowVictimOnMap>
    <HasCuriousNPC>true</HasCuriousNPC>
    <SuspectDescription>The dude is crazy, walks like a zombie and just killed someone!</SuspectDescription>
    <NoDetails>false</NoDetails>
    <HasCriminal>true</HasCriminal>
    <ChanceToCooperate>0</ChanceToCooperate>
    <CriminalPanicking>false</CriminalPanicking>
    <CriminalState>1</CriminalState>
    <HowInjured2></HowInjured2>
    <DrunkCriminal>true</DrunkCriminal>
    <HasStolenGoods>false</HasStolenGoods>
    <HasMeleeWeapon>true</HasMeleeWeapon>
    <HoldsMeleeWeapon>false</HoldsMeleeWeapon>
    <HasGun>false</HasGun>
    <HoldsGun>false</HoldsGun>
    <WontMove>false</WontMove>
    <HasDrugs>true</HasDrugs>
    <IsDrugDealer>false</IsDrugDealer>
    <IsWanted>true</IsWanted>
</Mission>

docs.title: EO - Enforcer Overhaul
docs.text: A light and simple overhaul mod for Enforcer: Police Crime Action that fixes a few issues of the game.

docs.title: Follow these steps:
docs.text: 1. Get MelonLoader v0.5.7 (x86). 2. After unzipping the downloaded file directly into the game's folder, launch the game. 3. Now close the game and download the mod. 4. Unzip the mod's contents into the Mods folder inside the game's folder, if the Mods folder isn't there, create it. 5. You did it! Run the game and have fun!
